prompt_text: Metabase vs Looker pricing and features
response:
Metabase is generally the **lower-cost, easier-to-use** option, while Looker is typically the **more expensive, enterprise-oriented** platform with a stronger semantic modeling layer and more governance depth.[2][5] If you want a practical comparison, Metabase is usually better for quick setup, self-serve analytics, and smaller teams; Looker is usually better when you need centralized data modeling, tighter governance, and large-scale BI standardization.[2][5]

| Category | **Metabase** | **Looker** |
|---|---|---|
| Pricing model | Open-source free tier; paid cloud/enterprise plans available.[2][3] | Custom pricing; reported starting points range from about **$3,000/month** to roughly **$35K/year historically**, with much higher enterprise spend possible.[1][5] |
| Cost profile | More affordable; Metabase says customers often pay roughly **one third** of what they paid for Looker.[2] | Usually substantially more expensive and often negotiated through sales.[1][5] |
| Ease of use | Designed to be intuitive for non-technical users; quick setup and low learning curve.[2][7] | More complex; steeper learning curve and often requires LookML expertise.[1][7] |
| Data modeling | Works well with SQL and external modeling tools like dbt; less proprietary modeling overhead.[2][7] | Strong **LookML** semantic layer for governed, reusable modeling.[1] |
| Dashboards & viz | Interactive dashboards, drill-through, charting, subscriptions, alerts.[2][6] | High-quality, interactive visualizations and dashboards.[1] |
| Governance / scale | Supports SSO and fine-grained permissions; positioned as affordable and scalable.[2][3] | Stronger enterprise governance and “single source of truth” positioning.[1] |
| Best fit | Teams wanting fast deployment, self-serve BI, and lower cost.[2][3] | Larger organizations needing centralized modeling and enterprise BI control.[1][5] |

**Metabase pricing:** Metabase offers an open-source version that is free, plus paid cloud/enterprise options for added security, governance, cloud hosting, and support.[2][3] One third-party source states Metabase Cloud can start at about **$85 per user monthly** for a Pro plan, but that figure is not from Metabase itself.[8]

**Looker pricing:** Looker does not publish simple fixed public pricing in the sources here; it is described as custom-priced, with one source citing about **$3,000/month starting** and another noting historical pricing around **$35K/year**, while real enterprise deals can be much higher depending on users, connections, and scale.[1][5] One source also reports add-on user pricing for viewers, standard users, and developers/admins in a marketplace-style listing.[5]

**Feature differences that matter most:**
- **Metabase** is easier for non-technical users to adopt, with a drag-and-drop query builder, quick dashboard creation, and straightforward sharing/alerts.[2][6][7]
- **Looker** is stronger if you want a governed modeling layer via **LookML**, especially in larger environments where consistent metric definitions matter.[1][5]
- **Metabase** is usually faster to stand up and more cost-effective for startups and growing teams.[2][3]
- **Looker** is usually the better fit for organizations that need more formal BI governance and are willing to pay for it.[1][5]

If you want, I can also give you a **“which should I choose?” recommendation by team size, budget, and use case**.
